behavior_summary: This script implements Darion's scheduling and fast-area behaviors: choosing a high-level U8P_DARION::schedule, storing it, and dispatching a runtime behavior when the player enters Darion's proximity (fast-area).

DARION::schedule (main) queries an external status table (FREE::28F9) to pick one of several U8P_DARION::schedule variants, spawns methods to install/run that U8P_DARION::schedule, and writes the chosen U8P_DARION::schedule id into the global darionSchedule slot.

U8P_DARION::enterFastArea resets stored reference slots for the NPC, checks death and global guard flags, and then either spawns a map-specific behavior or dispatches the runtime method that matches darionSchedule (for example U8P_DARION::02A8 for U8P_DARION::schedule==1 or U8P_DARION::0897 for other variants).
U8P_DARION::leaveFastArea cleans up by freeing the same reference slots when the player leaves the area.

U8P_DARION::02A8 is the active runtime loop for one U8P_DARION::schedule: it scans candidate items/targets, inspects item frames/status, pathfinds to targets, and triggers subordinate actions such as orientation, barks, and timers; it respects somebodyTalking to avoid interruptions.

U8P_DARION::0411 is the short interaction/bark routine: it chooses dialog lines (sometimes based on darionMet), orients Darion toward the target/player, plays a short animation or orientation method, and issues Item::bark to speak.
notable_control_flow: DARION::schedule early-exits if Npc::isDead returns true or global guards (secondexecution or toranDead) are set.

Schedule selection is data-driven: FREE::28F9 is queried with multiple indices and its returns determine which parameter block and U8P_DARION::schedule id are selected; the chosen id is then spawned via METHOD::143A and saved to darionSchedule before spawning METHOD::133F.

enterFastArea first clears reference slots (resetRef) to ensure a fresh state, then branches by map id (Npc::getMap) to choose special behavior (map==3 special-case) or read the global darionSchedule to decide which runtime method to spawn.

The runtime loop (U8P_DARION::02A8) iterates item lists, uses Item::getFrame / Item::getStatus to filter targets, calls Npc::pathfind to approach a chosen target (suspending until completion), and conditionally spawns U8P_DARION::0411 to handle barks when nobody is talking; fallback behavior spawns orientation/animation methods and timers via FREE::1C19.
leaveFastArea deterministically frees the references set/reset during enterFastArea (no complex branching).
open_questions: Exact numeric meanings of the U8P_DARION::schedule ids written into darionSchedule (what each id corresponds to in game-facing behavior beyond the inferred mapping).
Precise semantics and contents of the reference slots (0205, 0204, 00F0, 0200): what objects or processes they reference at runtime.
Full set of dialog strings and the exact conditions that choose each line (beyond the high-level use of darionMet and randomness).
Behavior of the special map==3 branch (what DARION::6227/DARION::0897 do in detail) and why devonInRule affects it.
Exact return/result convention for spawned METHOD::143A / METHOD::133F (what return values indicate success vs failure and how U8P_DARION::schedule() interprets them).

main_function.name: U8P_DARION::schedule
main_function.purpose: Choose and install Darion's high-level U8P_DARION::schedule/behavior variant by querying status data (FREE::28F9), spawn the installer method(s), and persist the chosen U8P_DARION::schedule id to the global darionSchedule so fast-area entry logic and runtime loops know which behavior to run.
entry_conditions: Invoked on Event 8 (scheduling event) to compute or re-compute Darion's U8P_DARION::schedule.
Runs only when Npc::isDead is false and global guards secondexecution and toranDead are not set (those cause an early exit).

function_map.name: U8P_DARION::schedule
function_map.role: Compute and install Darion's U8P_DARION::schedule; spawn installer methods and write darionSchedule.

function_map.name: U8P_DARION::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Handle player entering Darion's fast-area: reset refs, guard-check, branch by map or darionSchedule, and spawn the matching runtime behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_DARION::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Handle player leaving the fast-area: free/clear reference slots created/used while active.

function_map.name: U8P_DARION::02A8
function_map.role: Runtime behavior loop for a U8P_DARION::schedule variant: select targets, pathfind, spawn interaction barks/animations, and set timers.

function_map.name: U8P_DARION::0411
function_map.role: Short interaction/bark handler: pick a line (using randomness and darionMet), orient Darion, and call Item::bark.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine check used to avoid scheduling or behavior for a dead NPC.

function_map.name: FREE::28F9
function_map.role: External status/U8P_DARION::schedule lookup queried to decide which U8P_DARION::schedule variant to pick.

internal_calls.purpose: Not a direct call in U8P_DARION::schedule itself but related: the U8P_DARION::schedule value written by U8P_DARION::schedule() is later read by enterFastArea to dispatch runtime behavior. (Data-flow relation.)
Spawn the active runtime loop when darionSchedule==1 (dispatch runtime behavior upon fast-area entry).
Spawn alternate runtime behavior when darionSchedule indicates other variants (inferred mapping: U8P_DARION::schedule==3 or 4 -> 0897).
Spawn a short interaction/bark routine when nobody is talking or a target interaction is triggered.

external_calls.purpose: Guard: check if Darion is dead and early-exit scheduling if so.
Query configuration/status table with small indices to determine which U8P_DARION::schedule variant/parameter block to choose.
Spawn/install the chosen U8P_DARION::schedule behavior variant (installer/start routine).
Spawn a complementary/secondary method after installing the U8P_DARION::schedule (continuation or secondary behavior).
Initialize/clear stored reference slots for the NPC to ensure fresh state on area entry.
Free or clear the reference slots when leaving the fast-area.
Decide behavior by current map (special-case handling when map==3).
Set a map-specific activity/state for Darion (used in the map==3 branch).
Inspect candidate items' frames to filter/choose targets for interaction.
Move Darion to a chosen target; the routine suspends until the pathfind completes and result is inspected.
Spawn a timer/delay after actions (used to pace behavior after barks or movement).
Check item flags/status used to select dialog variants or special conditions for barks.
Pick a random variant of a speech line or action to vary dialog.
Emit the chosen dialog string to the player/viewer.

reads.name: secondexecution
reads.likely_effect: Acts as a guard preventing re-scheduling or re-dispatch when set; U8P_DARION::schedule() and enterFastArea early-exit if true.

reads.name: toranDead
reads.likely_effect: Global condition that blocks Darion's scheduling/behavior when set (early-exit guard).

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Used in enterFastArea with map==3 to select a special behavior variant for Darion (map-specific rule).

reads.name: darionSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Selector persisted by U8P_DARION::schedule() and read by enterFastArea to decide which runtime method to spawn on area entry.

reads.name: darionMet
reads.likely_effect: Dialog state read by U8P_DARION::0411 to prefer 'you're back' style greeting lines versus initial dialog.

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Concurrency guard read by U8P_DARION::02A8 to avoid speaking over other dialog; influences whether U8P_DARION::0411 is spawned.

writes.name: darionSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Written by DARION::schedule to persist the chosen U8P_DARION::schedule id so runtime and enterFastArea use the same behavior variant.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements Darion's scheduling and fast-area behaviors: choosing a high-level U8P_DARION::schedule, storing it, and dispatching a runtime behavior when the player enters Darion's proximity (fast-area).
